name: Nelson v. Colorado
question: Does the state of Colorado’s refusal to refund money from persons exonerated from convictions unless they can “prove, by clear and convincing evidence, that [they were] ‘actually innocent’” violate the Due Process Clause?
description: A case in which the Court decided that the Colorado Exoneration Act, which requires exonerated persons to “prove, by clear and convincing evidence, that they were ‘actually innocent’” in order to refund their money, does not comport with the Due Process Clause of the Fourteenth  Amendment.
